1.11a: Accounts starting 10013 or 10014: Accord Buy to Let, PO Box 869, Newport, NP20 9GY (Fax: 0345 848 0305).
All other accounts: Lending Shared Services Accord Mortgages Limited, Yorkshire House, Yorkshire Drive, Bradford BD5 8LJ (DX11798 Bradford).
1.11b: Pre-existing second charges must be removed before completion.
Existing mortgages must be fully repaid and Land Registry charges cancelled.
1.7: Lending is prohibited if the borrower and mortgagor are different parties.
1.14: Firm may act if a member is the seller provided it is a partnership and no conflict of interest exists.
The specific conveyancer handling the case cannot be the seller or an immediate family member of the seller.
1.15: Firm may act if a member is the borrower provided it is a partnership and no conflict of interest exists.
The specific conveyancer handling the case cannot be the borrower or an immediate family member of the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's name and address is not required.
3.1.4: Contact point for unrepresented sellers is same as 1.11a.
3.1.5: Acceptable identification: Full Driving Licence (photocard or old style paper version).
No documents are accepted from List B.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's name and address is not required.
3.2.4: Contact point for unrepresented sellers is same as 1.11a.
4.1: Valuation report is provided to the conveyancer with the mortgage offer.
4.3: Contact point for incorrect valuation assumptions is same as 1.11a.
4.5a: Contact point for re-inspection is same as 1.11a.
4.5b: Certificate of title should be sent to the address in 1.11a.
5.1.1: Contact point for properties owned less than 6 months is same as 1.11a.
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, assignable contracts, and irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are not accepted.
5.2.1: Contact point if seller is not the registered owner is same as 1.11a.
5.4.4: Do not send environmental or contaminated land reports to the lender.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if an unqualified Certificate of Title is provided.
Search firms must subscribe to the Search Code and be monitored by the Property Codes Compliance Board.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted if an unqualified Certificate of Title is provided.
5.5.3a: Contact point for breaches is same as 1.11a.
Indemnity insurance is permitted for planning/building regulation breaches 10+ years old if the valuation is not negative.
Breaches within enforcement periods require conveyancer recommendations and structural soundness confirmation; indemnity insurance alone is insufficient.
Private drainage must be compliant with current legislation; shared systems require legal access and maintenance arrangements.
HMOs and Multi Unit Blocks must possess all necessary licenses and consents.
5.5.3b: Original planning permissions are not required by the lender but must be kept on the conveyancer's file.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents are not required by the lender but must be kept on the conveyancer's file.
5.5.3d: Proof of residential status is required for commercial-to-residential conversions.
Certificate of lawful use is required only if planning permission for change of use cannot be confirmed.
5.5.4: Contact point for restrictive covenants is same as 1.11a.
5.7.1a: Lending on flying freeholds is not permitted.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is not permitted.
5.8.1: Lending accepted on buildings converted into max 4 flats where borrower occupies one and owns the freehold.
The borrower's flat must have a separate leasehold title.
Security must include the freehold title if owned by the borrower.
Lending is restricted to one flat per building.
5.8.5: Security must include the freehold reversion of other flats.
Shared freehold ownership requires a trust deed or confirmation that the share transfers simultaneously with the leasehold interest upon enforcement.
Tyneside flats are prohibited for resident landlords.
5.9.1: Lending on commonhold is not permitted.
5.10.1: Contact point for use restrictions is same as 1.11a.
5.13.1: Contact point for non-standard funding or second charges is same as 1.11a.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term must be 85 years from the date of completion.
5.14.9: Contact point for lease matters is same as 1.11a.
Maximum starting ground rent is £1,000 per annum.
Ground rent must be fixed for the first 21 years and reviewable no more than every 21 years thereafter.
Rent reviews capped at the higher of 100% of current rent or RPI increase.
5.14.10: Contact point for service charges is same as 1.11a.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms if it allows for an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.14.12: Ground rent/service charge receipts are not required by the lender but must be kept on file.
5.14.13: Notice of service evidence is not required by the lender but must be kept on file.
5.14.15a: Contact point for absentee/insolvent landlords is same as 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent/insolvent landlords is accepted if it allows for an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.14.17a: Documentation is not required prior to completion unless specified in 5.14.17b.
5.14.17b: Notify lender if remediation is required under the Building Safety Act 2022 or if valuer assumptions are incorrect.
Lending prohibited if fire safety works are required and non-qualifying leaseholders bear costs.
Written instructions from lender required before completion if safety issues are present.
5.14.17c: Remortgages must follow the same building safety instructions as purchases.
5.15.2a: Rentcharges/service charges must include 2 months' written notice of arrears to the lender before enforcement.
Enforcement leases must be surrenderable upon payment of reasonable costs with no premium.
Maximum annual rentcharge/freehold service charge is £500.
Charges must not double more than once every 25 years.
Indemnity insurance is required for uncollected historic rentcharges (12+ years old) not related to services.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates are not required.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association are not required.
5.16.2: Contact point for search entry certification is same as 1.11a.
5.17.5a: Power of Attorney documents do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations of non-revocation do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.19.1: Lending is not permitted for affordable housing, key worker schemes, shared equity, or shared ownership.
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel leases that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements to the contact in 1.11a.
5.20.3: Solar PV lease break clauses must state the provider is responsible for removal costs and damage repair if the lender terminates the lease.
5.20.4: Disclose any Green Deal Plan details to the contact in 1.11a.
6.1.3: Contact point for aborted mortgages is same as 1.11a.
6.2.1: Contact point for property description discrepancies is same as 1.11a.
6.3.1: Contact point for purchase price issues is same as 1.11a.
6.4.4: All incentives, discounts, and cashbacks must be reported.
6.4.5: Contact point if purchase money control is limited is same as 1.11a.
6.5.1: Contact point for vacant possession issues is same as 1.11a.
6.6.1: Contact point for BTL letting queries is same as 1.11a.
Property must have a minimum EPC rating of E at completion unless a valid PRS exemption exists.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements are not required by the lender but must be kept on file.
6.6.4: Lending prohibited if a mandatory HMO license is required.
Lending allowed for houses with additional, selective, or discretionary licensing if the license is in place by completion.
6.7.1: Accepted warranties: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, LABC, Checkmate/Castle 10 (with garage endorsement), Build-Zone, Build Assure, Global Home Warranties, Q Policy (Q Assure Build), Protek, Advantage, ICW (flat roof >10sqm endorsement), Ark Residential (garage endorsement), One Guarantee, TMSC, HomeProof.
6.7.2: New home warranty documents are not required by the lender but must be kept on file.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes are not required by the lender but must be kept on file.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant's Certificates (PCC) must use the unmodified UK Finance form.
PCCs accepted for max 4 units in a continuous structure and max 10 units in a development.
Retrospective PCCs are not accepted; projects must be supervised and inspected from the start.
6.7.6: PCCs do not need to be sent to the lender but must be kept on file.
6.8.1: Agreements for unadopted roads/sewers are not required by the lender.
6.9.1: Contact point for easement issues is same as 1.11a.
Indemnity insurance is required if title defects cannot be rectified.
If access depends on the borrower's adjoining land, that land must also be charged to the lender.
6.10.2: Retentions are released to the borrower unless otherwise authorized.
Advance instalments are released to the conveyancer.
6.11.1: Contact point for redevelopment/road proposals is same as 1.11a.
6.12.1: Contact point for resale restrictions or options is same as 1.11a.
6.13.1: Contact point for improvement/repair grants is same as 1.11a.
7.3: Occupant consent is not required for BTL, but letting to the borrower's family is prohibited.
7.4: Contact point for disclosure accuracy doubts is same as 1.11a.
8.1: Conveyancers may advise third parties if no conflict of interest exists and the document is not vulnerable to challenge.
9.1: Indemnity policies do not need to be sent to the lender but must be kept on file.
9.2: Indemnity limit must at least equal the mortgage advance and cover borrowers, successors, and the mortgagee.
10.2a: Advance is paid electronically via CHAPS.
10.2b: Certificate of Title must be received at least 6 working days before completion.
Funds are released on the working day before completion.
10.3: Standard deductions include a Funds Transfer Fee and potentially a Higher Lending Charge (refer to offer).
10.7: Funds must be returned within one working day of the scheduled completion date.
Case IDs starting 'M': NatWest, Sort 56-00-36, Acc 28512162.
Account number and borrower name must be quoted or interest will be charged.
10.11: Delayed return of funds incurs interest at the mortgage rate.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application required for instalment mortgages or retentions.
14.1.5: If using e-DRS: Send original mortgage deed to lender and keep a certified copy on file.
If not using e-DRS: Lodge original mortgage deed with the Land Registry.
